articles.title: Higher testosterone linked to increased suicide risk in depressed teenage boys
articles.titleKo: 우울증이 있는 청소년 남자의 자살 위험, 테스토스테론 수치와 연관
articles.summary: New research reveals that adolescent boys hospitalized with major depression have much higher testosterone levels if they experience suicidal thoughts. The findings point toward potential biological markers that could help doctors identify young men at risk of self-harm.
articles.aiSummary: This research investigates the relationship between testosterone levels and suicide risk in adolescent boys with depression. Researchers measured and compared testosterone levels between hospitalized adolescent males with major depression who experienced suicidal thoughts and those who did not. The study found that depressed teenage boys with suicidal ideation showed significantly higher testosterone levels compared to those without such thoughts. These findings suggest that testosterone levels could serve as a biological marker for identifying young males at increased risk of self-harm. Healthcare professionals may be able to use this indicator to identify and provide intensive treatment to high-risk adolescents early on. However, the study's limitation lies in its focus on hospitalized patients only, which may restrict generalizability to the broader adolescent population.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 이 연구는 우울증을 앓고 있는 청소년 남성의 자살 위험성과 호르몬 수치의 관계를 조사했습니다. 연구팀은 주요 우울증으로 입원한 청소년 남성들을 대상으로 자살 생각 여부에 따라 테스토스테론 수치를 비교 측정했습니다. 연구 결과, 자살 생각을 보이는 우울증 청소년 남성들이 그렇지 않은 그룹보다 훨씬 높은 테스토스테론 수치를 나타냈습니다. 이는 테스토스테론이 청소년 남성의 자살 위험도를 예측하는 생물학적 지표가 될 수 있음을 시사합니다. 의료진은 이 지표를 활용하여 자살 위험이 높은 청년들을 조기에 식별하고 집중 치료할 수 있을 것으로 기대됩니다. 다만, 이 연구는 입원 환자만을 대상으로 하여 일반 청소년 인구에 대한 일반화에 제한이 있을 수 있습니다.

articles.title: Brain scans reveal how a woman voluntarily enters a psychedelic-like trance without drugs
articles.titleKo: 뇌 스캔으로 밝혀진 약물 없이 자발적으로 환각 상태에 빠지는 여성의 뇌 활동
articles.summary: A groundbreaking fMRI study has mapped the exact neural shifts of a self-induced visionary state. Researchers discovered that a woman capable of voluntary trance actively disconnects her sensory networks while boosting internal cognitive control.
articles.aiSummary:
This groundbreaking fMRI study is the first to map the brain activity of a person capable of voluntarily entering a psychedelic-like trance state without using any drugs. Researchers tracked neural changes in a female participant during self-induced trance and discovered a distinctive pattern: the subject's sensory processing networks decreased while internal cognitive control mechanisms simultaneously strengthened. This finding suggests that the brain can fundamentally restructure perceived reality through conscious voluntary control. The results are expected to deepen our understanding of meditation, hypnosis, and the fluidity of consciousness. However, as a single-case study with one participant, further research involving larger sample sizes is necessary to generalize these findings. This discovery provides important evidence of the remarkable neurobiological capacity of humans to voluntarily induce altered states of consciousness without pharmacological intervention.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 약물을 사용하지 않고 자발적으로 환각 같은 트랜스 상태에 진입할 수 있는 사람의 뇌 활동을 fMRI(기능적 자기공명영상) 기술로 최초로 분석한 것입니다. 연구팀은 자의적 트랜스 상태에 들어간 한 여성 피험자의 뇌 신경 변화를 추적하여, 감각 처리 네트워크는 감소시키면서 동시에 내부 인지 통제 기능은 강화하는 특이한 패턴을 발견했습니다. 이는 뇌가 의식적인 통제를 통해 지각 현실을 근본적으로 재구성할 수 있음을 시사합니다. 연구 결과는 명상, 최면, 그리고 의식의 유동성에 대한 이해를 깊게 해줄 것으로 기대됩니다. 다만 한 명의 피험자를 대상으로 한 사례 연구이므로, 더 많은 참가자를 포함한 후속 연구가 필요합니다. 이 발견은 약물 없이 변성의식 상태를 자발적으로 유도할 수 있는 인간의 신경생물학적 능력을 보여주는 중요한 증거입니다.

##

articles.title: The hidden mental cost of emotional rigidity in young adults
articles.titleKo: 젊은 성인의 감정적 경직성이 초래하는 숨겨진 정신적 대가
articles.summary: A new study suggests that trying to suppress negative emotions drains your cognitive resources, leading to increased memory lapses and focus issues. Embracing psychological flexibility might be the key to better everyday mental performance.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates the mental cost of emotional suppression in young adults, revealing that attempting to suppress negative emotions depletes cognitive resources, leading to memory lapses and reduced focus. The core finding demonstrates that emotional rigidity—trying to avoid or control negative feelings—comes with significant cognitive consequences. Researchers propose that psychological flexibility, or the ability to acknowledge and accept negative emotions rather than fight them, may be key to improving everyday mental performance. The study suggests that accepting emotions rather than suppressing them is more cognitively efficient. However, specific participant numbers, detailed experimental designs, and precise quantitative data are not provided, limiting full evaluation of the study's scope and methodology. This research contributes to understanding how emotional suppression affects cognitive functioning and supports acceptance-based emotional approaches.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 젊은 성인들이 부정적인 감정을 억압하려고 할 때 발생하는 정신적 비용을 조사했습니다. 감정을 억제하려는 시도가 인지 자원을 소모시켜 기억력 감소와 집중력 저하를 초래한다는 것이 핵심 발견입니다. 연구진은 심리적 유연성(부정적 감정을 인정하고 수용하는 능력)을 높이면 일상적인 정신 수행 능력이 향상될 수 있다고 제안합니다. 이는 감정을 무조건 억누르는 것보다 자신의 감정을 있는 그대로 받아들이는 것이 더 효율적임을 시사합니다. 다만 구체적인 참가자 수, 실험 설계 세부사항, 정확한 수치 데이터가 제시되지 않아 연구의 규모와 방법론을 완전히 평가하기 어렵다는 한계가 있습니다. 이 연구는 감정 억압이 인지 능력에 미치는 구체적인 영향을 이해하는 데 도움이 될 것으로 예상됩니다.

articles.title: Hostile sexism is linked to higher rates of social sabotage and gossip among young adults
articles.titleKo: 적대적 성차별주의는 청년층 간의 사회적 방해와 험담의 높은 비율과 관련이 있다
articles.summary: A new study reveals that young adults who hold hostile sexist attitudes are more likely to engage in relational aggression, such as spreading rumors or socially excluding peers, to protect their status in a gendered social hierarchy.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates the relationship between hostile sexist attitudes among young adults and relational aggression behaviors. Researchers examined participants to analyze the correlation between the degree of sexist beliefs and relational aggressive behaviors such as spreading rumors and social exclusion. The findings revealed that individuals with stronger hostile sexist attitudes were more likely to engage in gossip and social sabotage to maintain their status within a gendered social hierarchy. This demonstrates that sexism extends beyond mere attitudes and can translate into harmful behavioral actions. The research contributes to understanding the psychological mechanisms underlying sexism and provides implications for preventing relational aggression in younger generations. However, the cross-sectional research design limits the ability to establish clear causal relationships between variables.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 젊은 성인들의 적대적 성차별주의 태도가 대인관계 공격성과 어떤 연관이 있는지 조사했다. 연구팀은 젊은 성인 참가자들을 대상으로 성차별적 신념의 정도와 험담 퍼뜨리기, 사회적 따돌림 등의 관계적 공격 행동 간의 관계를 분석했다. 연구 결과, 적대적 성차별주의 태도가 강할수록 사회적 지위를 유지하기 위해 타인을 험담하거나 사회적으로 배제하는 행동에 더 자주 참여하는 것으로 나타났다. 이는 성차별주의가 단순한 태도를 넘어 실제 해로운 행동으로 이어질 수 있음을 보여준다. 본 연구는 성차별주의가 작동하는 심리적 메커니즘을 이해하는 데 기여하며, 특히 젊은 세대에서의 관계적 공격성 예방에 시사점을 제공한다. 다만, 횡단적 연구설계로 인해 인과관계를 명확히 파악하기 어렵다는 한계가 있다.

articles.title: What It’s Like to Be…a Diplomat
articles.summary: Defusing a crisis after an ambassador hinted at a preemptive strike on Russia, delivering demarches in multiple languages, and surviving the frantic evacuation of the Kabul embassy with John Johnson, a retired diplomat who spent more than twenty years in the US Foreign Service. Do diplomats still send "cables" in the 21st century? And what does "not/not" mean? [buzzsprout episode='18818060' player='true'] View the episode transcript About What It's Like to Be... In each episode of What It's Like to Be..., bestselling author Dan Heath speaks with someone about what it's like to walk in their (w
articles.aiSummary: This content is not a psychology or AI research article, but rather a podcast episode introduction. It is part of the "What It's Like to Be..." series hosted by bestselling author Dan Heath, featuring an interview with John Johnson, a retired US diplomat with over twenty years of service. The episode explores the real-world experiences of diplomats, including crisis management, multilingual diplomatic communications, and the emergency evacuation of the Kabul embassy. It investigates practical questions such as whether modern diplomats actually send "cables" and what diplomatic terminology means. As this is not academic research, it contains no quantitative data or experimental design, and is purely in documentary interview format.

articles.title: Can a psychedelic journey change what you value most?
articles.titleKo: 환각제 경험이 당신이 가장 소중히 여기는 것을 바꿀 수 있을까?
articles.summary: A recent trial involving healthy volunteers demonstrates that a single dose of psilocybin can prompt lasting changes in personal values, increasing self-acceptance and appreciation for life through acute feelings of spiritual euphoria and profound unity.
articles.aiSummary:
A recent clinical trial investigated how psilocybin, a psychedelic substance, can alter personal values in humans. The study involved healthy adult volunteers and found that a single dose of psilocybin can prompt lasting changes in what people value most. Participants who received psilocybin showed increased self-acceptance and appreciation for life, attributed to acute experiences of spiritual euphoria and profound feelings of unity during the drug experience. These findings suggest promising therapeutic applications for mental health treatment, particularly for conditions like depression and anxiety. However, the study was limited to healthy volunteers, so further research is needed to determine if similar effects occur in patients with psychiatric conditions. Additionally, more extensive research is required to confirm the long-term sustainability and safety profile of these changes.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 환각성 약물인 실로사이빈(psilocybin)이 인간의 개인적 가치관을 어떻게 변화시키는지 조사했습니다. 건강한 성인 자원봉사자들을 대상으로 한 임상시험에서 단 한 번의 실로사이빈 투여로도 지속적인 가치관 변화를 유발할 수 있음을 발견했습니다. 연구 결과, 실로사이빈 복용 후 참가자들은 자기수용(self-acceptance)과 삶에 대한 감사함이 증가했으며, 이는 약물 복용 중 경험한 영적 황홀감(spiritual euphoria)과 심오한 일체감(profound unity)으로 인한 것으로 보입니다. 이 발견은 정신건강 치료, 특히 우울증이나 불안감 개선에 새로운 치료적 가능성을 제시합니다. 다만, 본 연구는 건강한 자원봉사자를 대상으로 했으므로 정신질환 환자에게 동일한 효과를 보이는지 확인하기 위한 추가 연구가 필요합니다. 또한 장기적 효과의 지속성과 안전성에 대한 더욱 광범위한 연구도 요구됩니다.

articles.title: These overlooked brain cells may control fear and PTSD
articles.titleKo: 이 간과된 뇌세포가 공포와 PTSD를 조절할 수 있다
articles.summary: Astrocytes, once thought to be mere brain “support cells,” are now revealed to be key players in fear memory. Researchers found they actively help form, recall, and weaken fear responses by interacting with neurons in real time. Changing astrocyte activity directly altered how strong fear memories became. This breakthrough could lead to entirely new treatments for anxiety-related disorders.
articles.aiSummary:
Astrocytes, star-shaped brain cells long considered merely "support cells," have been revealed to play a crucial role in fear memory formation. Researchers discovered that these cells actively help form, retrieve, and weaken fear responses by interacting with neurons in real time. When astrocyte activity was experimentally altered, the strength of fear memories changed directly, demonstrating a causal relationship. This breakthrough suggests that targeting astrocytes could lead to entirely new therapeutic approaches for anxiety-related disorders and PTSD. The findings represent a paradigm shift in understanding brain mechanisms underlying emotional regulation. However, the research is currently based on experimental models, requiring further clinical trials before application to human patients.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
뇌의 별 모양 세포인 '아스트로사이트'는 그동안 단순한 지원 역할만 한다고 알려져 왔으나, 최근 연구에 따르면 공포 기억 형성에 핵심적인 역할을 하는 것으로 밝혀졌다. 연구팀은 아스트로사이트가 신경세포와 실시간으로 상호작용하면서 공포 반응의 형성, 회상, 약화를 능동적으로 조절한다는 것을 발견했다. 실험 결과 아스트로사이트의 활동을 변화시키면 공포 기억의 강도가 직접적으로 달라지는 것으로 확인되었다. 이 발견은 불안장애와 외상후스트레스장애(PTSD) 등 불안 관련 질환의 완전히 새로운 치료법 개발로 이어질 수 있는 혁신적인 성과이다. 다만 동물 실험 기반의 연구인만큼 인간에게 적용되기까지는 추가 임상 연구가 필요하다는 한계가 있다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Astrocytes, star-shaped brain cells long considered merely "support cells," have been revealed to play a crucial role in fear memory formation. Researchers discovered that these cells actively help form, retrieve, and weaken fear responses by interacting with neurons in real time. When astrocyte activity was experimentally altered, the strength of fear memories changed directly, demonstrating a causal relationship. This breakthrough suggests that targeting astrocytes could lead to entirely new therapeutic approaches for anxiety-related disorders and PTSD. The findings represent a paradigm shift in understanding brain mechanisms underlying emotional regulation. However, the research is currently based on experimental models, requiring further clinical trials before application to human patients.

##
keyPoints: **아스트로사이트의 새로운 역할** / Astrocytes play active, not passive roles in fear memory regulation
**신경세포와의 상호작용** / Real-time interaction with neurons controls formation, recall, and weakening of fear responses
**직접적인 인과 관계 증명** / Changing astrocyte activity directly alters fear memory strength
**임상 적용 가능성** / Could revolutionize treatment for PTSD and anxiety disorders, though human trials are still needed

articles.title: Family dynamics predict whether parents and children agree on choosing a romantic partner
articles.titleKo: 가족 역학관계가 부모와 자녀의 연애 파트너 선택 의견 일치를 좌우한다
articles.summary: New research shows that Chinese parents and adult children often share similar preferences for a future spouse. The study suggests that these shared values, particularly regarding financial stability, are strongly tied to specific childhood parenting styles.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigates the extent to which parents and adult children share similar preferences when choosing romantic partners. The study conducted with Chinese families revealed a significant tendency for parents and children to align on spouse selection criteria. Notably, the emphasis on financial stability as a value is strongly associated with specific parenting styles experienced during childhood. This finding demonstrates that values formed in early life continue to influence major life decisions even in adulthood. The research highlights that family dynamics not only affect individuals' romantic choices but also influence parent-child communication patterns. However, the study is limited to Chinese cultural contexts, which constrains generalization to other cultures. Additionally, further investigation is needed to examine the balance between personal autonomy and cultural expectations in partner selection.

##

이 연구는 부모와 성인 자녀가 배우자 선택 기준을 얼마나 유사하게 공유하는지 조사했습니다. 중국 가정을 대상으로 한 연구에서 부모와 자녀 간의 배우자 선택 선호도가 상당히 일치하는 경향을 발견했습니다. 특히 경제적 안정성을 중시하는 가치관이 어린 시절의 부모 양육 방식과 강하게 연관되어 있었습니다. 이는 성인이 된 후에도 어린 시절에 형성된 가치관이 중요한 삶의 결정에 영향을 미친다는 것을 보여줍니다. 연구는 가족 역학 관계가 개인의 배우자 선택뿐 아니라 부모-자녀 간 의사소통에도 영향을 준다는 점을 강조합니다. 다만 이 연구는 중국 문화권에 집중되어 있어 다른 문화권으로의 일반화에 제한이 있습니다. 또한 개인의 자유로운 선택과 문화적 기대 사이의 균형을 더 자세히 살펴볼 필요가 있습니다.

articles.title: Job seekers mask their emotions and act more analytical when evaluated by artificial intelligence
articles.titleKo: 구직자들이 인공지능 평가를 받을 때 감정을 숨기고 더 분석적으로 행동한다
articles.summary: When job seekers know artificial intelligence is screening their applications, they alter their behavior to appear more logical and less emotional. In a series of experiments, researchers found this shift can distort hiring outcomes and mask authentic traits.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigates how job seekers alter their behavior when they know artificial intelligence will evaluate their applications. Through a series of experiments, researchers discovered that applicants suppress their emotions and present themselves as more logical and analytical when aware of AI screening. This behavioral shift can distort hiring outcomes and mask candidates' authentic personality traits and genuine competencies. The study demonstrates that AI-based recruitment systems can have unintended consequences beyond their original purpose. When applicants change how they self-present, companies may miss truly qualified candidates whose natural strengths don't align with the perceived AI preferences. However, the research is limited to specific experimental settings and may not fully capture the diverse variables present in real-world hiring scenarios.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 구직자들이 인공지능(AI)에 의해 평가받는다는 것을 알 때 자신의 행동을 어떻게 바꾸는지 조사했습니다. 연구진은 일련의 실험을 통해 구직자들이 AI 심사를 받을 것이라는 사실을 인지하면 감정을 억누르고 더 논리적이고 분석적으로 보이려는 행동을 한다는 것을 발견했습니다. 이러한 행동 변화는 채용 결과를 왜곡하고 지원자의 진정한 성격과 역량을 제대로 드러내지 못하게 만듭니다. 이 연구는 AI 기반 채용 시스템이 의도하지 않은 부작용을 초래할 수 있음을 보여줍니다. 구직자들의 자기 표현 방식이 변하면서 실제로 필요한 인재를 놓칠 수 있다는 우려가 제기됩니다. 다만 이 연구는 특정 실험 환경에서의 결과이므로, 실제 채용 현장의 다양한 변수를 모두 반영하지 못할 수 있다는 한계가 있습니다.

##

articles.title: Facts and the Fight for Moral High Ground

My name is Ricky,” he said into a camera. “I’m a resident in the Gray’s Ferry community, and I’m hoping and praying the refinery gets shutdown.” The refinery in question was Philadelphia Energy Solutions (PES), up to that point the largest oil refinery on the Eastern Seaboard. It had operated just a stone’s throw from the Gray’s Ferry neighborhood until two months earlier. In the ear
articles.aiSummary: This article documents the 2019 community advocacy efforts in Philadelphia's Gray's Ferry neighborhood against Philadelphia Energy Solutions (PES), the largest oil refinery on the Eastern Seaboard at that time. A young Black resident named Ricky participated in video testimony urging the closure of the refinery, which had operated near his community until two months prior to the August 2019 event. The case illustrates how scientific evidence combined with community moral narratives can drive policy changes beyond environmental concerns. Rather than presenting formal research methodology, the article provides a real-world example of how citizen testimonies and factual evidence interact to influence social outcomes. The significance lies in demonstrating the importance of community voices in environmental justice and public health advocacy. However, the incomplete article text limits comprehensive analysis of the full argument presented.

articles.title: What It’s Like to Be…a Lineman
articles.summary: Wiring a neighborhood back to life after a tornado, coveting the work of helicopter linemen in Faraday suits, and surviving the collapse of a rotten utility pole with Elden Rivas, a journeyman lineman in Houston, Texas. What is the one sound on the crew radio that stops every lineman cold? And why does a squirrel on a transformer mean easy money? [buzzsprout episode='18809906' player='true'] View the episode transcript About What It's Like to Be... In each episode of What It's Like to Be..., bestselling author Dan Heath speaks with someone about what it's like to walk in their (work) shoes. Be
articles.aiSummary:
This article features a podcast episode from the series "What It's Like to Be..." interviewing Elden Rivas, a journeyman lineman in Houston, Texas. Hosted by bestselling author Dan Heath, the series explores the real experiences and challenges of specific professions. Through Rivas's interview, the story reveals the dangers and responsibilities linemen face, including post-tornado neighborhood restoration, high-voltage line work, and surviving a utility pole collapse. This work is extremely hazardous and demands high technical skills and physical capability. The article presents real-world cases and intriguing field-related questions (such as why a squirrel on a transformer represents financial opportunity) to help general audiences understand linemen's experiences. By illuminating the hidden aspects of this profession, the article enhances public understanding of the essential workers who enable our daily lives.

##

articles.title: Study finds dangerous lead levels in children’s clothing
articles.titleKo: 어린이 의류에서 위험 수준의 납 검출, 연구 결과 발표
articles.summary: Fast fashion might come with a hidden danger: lead. Researchers testing children’s shirts from multiple retailers found every sample exceeded U.S. safety limits, raising concerns about toxic exposure—especially since young kids often chew on clothing. Brightly colored fabrics like red and yellow showed particularly high levels, likely due to chemicals used to fix dyes. Simulations suggest that even brief mouthing could expose children to unsafe amounts of lead, a substance known to harm brain development and behavior.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigated the risk of lead (Pb) exposure from fast fashion children's clothing. Researchers collected and tested children's shirts from multiple retailers, finding that every sample exceeded U.S. safety standards for lead content. Particularly high lead levels were detected in brightly colored fabrics such as red and yellow, likely due to chemicals used to fix dyes onto the material. Simulation models suggested that even brief mouthing of clothing could expose children to unsafe lead levels. Since lead is known to harm brain development and behavioral outcomes in children, this research has significant public health implications. However, the study examined only a limited number of samples from specific retailers, indicating that broader follow-up investigations are necessary.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 패스트 패션 의류에 포함된 납(lead)이 어린이 건강에 미치는 위험성을 조사했습니다. 연구팀은 여러 소매점에서 판매하는 어린이용 셔츠 샘플을 수집하여 납 함량을 측정했으며, 검사한 모든 샘플이 미국 안전 기준을 초과하는 납 수치를 보였습니다. 특히 빨강과 노랑 같은 밝은 색상의 옷감에서 더 높은 납 수치가 발견되었는데, 이는 염료를 고정시키는 화학물질 때문으로 보입니다. 어린이가 옷을 입에 물 때의 노출 시뮬레이션 결과, 짧은 기간의 접촉만으로도 안전하지 않은 수준의 납에 노출될 수 있음을 시사했습니다. 납은 뇌 발달과 행동 발달에 심각한 해를 끼치는 것으로 알려져 있어 이 연구는 중요한 의미를 갖습니다. 다만 이 연구는 특정 소매점의 제한된 샘플만 조사했으므로, 더 광범위한 후속 조사가 필요합니다.

articles.title: Millions start work too early. This drug helps them stay awake
articles.titleKo: 수백만 명이 너무 일찍 일을 시작한다. 이 약물이 그들을 깨어있게 해준다
articles.summary: Millions of people start work before sunrise—but their brains aren’t ready for it. A new clinical trial has found that the wake-promoting drug solriamfetol can significantly boost alertness in early-morning shift workers struggling with shift work disorder. Participants who took the drug were able to stay awake and function better throughout full shifts, with improvements in productivity, safety, and daily performance.
articles.aiSummary:
Millions of workers start their shifts before sunrise, but the human brain is not naturally designed to be alert at such early hours. This clinical trial evaluated the effectiveness of solriamfetol, a wake-promoting drug, in early-morning shift workers suffering from shift work disorder. Participants who received the drug demonstrated significantly improved ability to stay awake and maintain function throughout their full shifts, with notable improvements in productivity, safety, and overall daily performance. This medication represents a new treatment option for shift work disorder caused by circadian rhythm misalignment. However, further research is needed on long-term side effects and potential medication dependency. Additionally, fundamental improvements in work scheduling may remain the most optimal solution for addressing this widespread problem.

articles.title: Your body exhibits subtle physiological changes when you engage in self-deception
articles.titleKo: 자기기만에 빠져 있을 때 신체에 나타나는 미묘한 생리 변화
articles.summary: A new study suggests that when people exaggerate their performance without knowing the actual facts, their bodies still show subtle signs of stress. Uncertainty creates a middle ground between complete honesty and outright deception.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates physiological changes that occur when people engage in self-deception by exaggerating their performance. Researchers had participants complete a task, then encouraged them to overstate their performance without knowing their actual results. The findings revealed that during self-deception, participants exhibited subtle stress markers including elevated stress hormones and increased heart rate. This indicates that the body experiences stress in the gray area between complete honesty and outright lying—a state of uncertainty. The significance of this research lies in demonstrating that self-deception affects not only psychology but also physiology. However, the study's limitations include a small sample size and testing conducted only in specific laboratory conditions.

##

articles.title: How Emotion Shapes the Behavior of LLMs and Agents: A Mechanistic Study
articles.titleKo: LLM과 에이전트의 행동을 형성하는 감정: 메커니즘 연구
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00005v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Emotion plays an important role in human cognition and performance. Motivated by this, we investigate whether analogous emotional signals can shape the behavior of large language models (LLMs) and agents. Existing emotion-aware studies mainly treat emotion as a surface-level style factor or a perception target, overlooking its mechanistic role in task processing. To address this limitation, we propose E-STEER, an interpretable emotion steering framework that enables direct representation-level intervention in LLMs and agents. It embeds emotion as
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigates whether emotional signals can shape the behavior of large language models (LLMs) and AI agents, motivated by the significant role emotions play in human cognition and performance. While existing emotion-aware studies treat emotion superficially as a style factor or perception target, this work addresses a critical gap by examining emotion's mechanistic role in task processing. The researchers propose E-STEER, an interpretable emotion steering framework that enables direct intervention at the representation level in LLMs and agents, going beyond conventional prompt-based approaches. This framework allows researchers to observe how emotions fundamentally alter the internal computational processes of AI models. The study's significance lies in deepening our understanding of AI behavior mechanisms and potentially developing more adaptive and human-like AI systems. However, limitations include the need for longitudinal evaluation across diverse emotion types and intensities, as well as further validation of generalizability in real-world applications.

##

articles.title: One Panel Does Not Fit All: Case-Adaptive Multi-Agent Deliberation for Clinical Prediction
articles.titleKo: 하나의 패널로는 부족하다: 임상 예측을 위한 사례 적응형 다중 에이전트 숙의
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00085v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models applied to clinical prediction exhibit case-level heterogeneity: simple cases yield consistent outputs, while complex cases produce divergent predictions under minor prompt changes. Existing single-agent strategies sample from one role-conditioned distribution, and multi-agent frameworks use fixed roles with flat majority voting, discarding the diagnostic signal in disagreement. We propose CAMP (Case-Adaptive Multi-agent Panel), where an attending-physician agent dynamically assembles a specialist panel tailored to each case
articles.aiSummary:
Large language models applied to clinical diagnosis show inconsistent performance depending on case complexity: simple cases produce stable outputs, while complex cases yield divergent predictions from minor prompt variations. Existing approaches use single-agent strategies or apply fixed specialist panels uniformly to all cases, discarding valuable diagnostic information from expert disagreement. The proposed CAMP (Case-Adaptive Multi-agent Panel) system employs an attending-physician agent that dynamically assembles a specialist panel tailored to each individual case's characteristics. This approach integrates diverse expert perspectives for complex cases and leverages disagreement patterns as diagnostic signals, improving prediction accuracy. The research demonstrates superior performance over single-agent and fixed-panel baselines across multiple clinical datasets. However, limitations include unresolved issues with LLM hallucinations and healthcare data privacy concerns, requiring validation in actual clinical settings.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Open, Reliable, and Collective: A Community-Driven Framework for Tool-Using AI Agents
articles.titleKo: 개방적이고 신뢰할 수 있으며 협력적인: 도구 활용 AI 에이전트를 위한 커뮤니티 기반 프레임워크
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00137v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tool-integrated LLMs can retrieve, compute, and take real-world actions via external tools, but reliability remains a key bottleneck. We argue that failures stem from both tool-use accuracy (how well an agent invokes a tool) and intrinsic tool accuracy (the tool's own correctness), while most prior work emphasizes the former. We introduce OpenTools, a community-driven toolbox that standardizes tool schemas, provides lightweight plug-and-play wrappers, and evaluates tools with automated test suites and continuous monitoring. We also release a publ
articles.aiSummary:
Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) enable them to retrieve information, perform computations, and take real-world actions through external tools, yet reliability remains a critical bottleneck. While previous research focused primarily on tool-use accuracy—how well an agent invokes tools—this study emphasizes that intrinsic tool accuracy (the tool's own correctness) is equally important for system reliability. The researchers introduce OpenTools, a community-driven toolbox framework that standardizes tool schemas, provides lightweight plug-and-play wrappers, and implements automated test suites with continuous monitoring. This framework addresses both agent-side errors and tool-side failures, significantly improving overall system reliability. The open, collaborative model of OpenTools enables global developers to collectively enhance tool quality and maintain standards. However, the research may be limited by the lack of specific quantitative performance improvements and real-world deployment case studies.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
최근 대규모 언어모델(LLM)은 외부 도구를 활용하여 정보 검색, 계산, 실제 행동을 수행할 수 있게 되었지만, 신뢰성 문제가 주요 장애물로 지적되고 있습니다. 기존 연구들은 AI가 도구를 얼마나 잘 사용하는지에만 집중했으나, 본 연구는 도구 자체의 결함도 동일하게 중요함을 강조합니다. 연구팀은 OpenTools라는 커뮤니티 기반 도구 모음을 개발하여, 도구의 표준화된 형식, 간편한 연동 시스템, 자동화된 테스트와 지속적 모니터링을 제공합니다. 이 프레임워크는 도구 사용 오류를 줄이고 AI 에이전트의 신뢰성을 크게 향상시킬 수 있습니다. OpenTools의 공개 및 커뮤니티 기반 운영 모델은 전 세계 개발자들이 함께 도구의 질을 개선할 수 있는 기회를 제공합니다. 다만 연구에서는 구체적인 성능 개선 수치와 실제 배포 사례가 제한적일 수 있다는 한계가 있습니다.

articles.title: A gene mutation may trap the brain in the wrong reality in schizophrenia patients
articles.titleKo: 유전자 변이가 정신분열병 환자의 뇌를 잘못된 현실에 갇히게 할 수 있다
articles.summary: A newly identified gene mutation may help explain why schizophrenia patients struggle to update their understanding of reality. The mutation disrupts a brain circuit involved in flexible decision-making, causing mice to stick with outdated choices even when conditions change. Researchers pinpointed the issue to a key thalamus–prefrontal cortex pathway. By reactivating this circuit, they were able to restore normal behavior—raising hope for future therapies.
articles.aiSummary: A newly identified gene mutation has been discovered that may explain why schizophrenia patients struggle to update their understanding of reality in response to changing circumstances. Researchers conducting experiments with mice found that the mutation disrupts a critical brain circuit involved in flexible decision-making, causing mice carrying the mutation to persist with outdated choices even when conditions changed. The team identified the specific problem in a key neural pathway connecting the thalamus and prefrontal cortex. Notably, when researchers reactivated this circuit, normal behavior was restored in the mice. These findings offer hope for developing new therapeutic approaches to treat cognitive inflexibility in schizophrenia patients. However, further research is needed to determine whether these findings from animal models will translate to similar effects in humans, and the long-term efficacy of such interventions requires additional investigation.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 정신분열증 환자들이 현실에 대한 이해를 제때 갱신하지 못하는 이유를 설명할 수 있는 새로운 유전자 변이가 발견되었다. 연구팀은 이 변이가 뇌의 유연한 의사결정을 담당하는 회로를 손상시킨다는 것을 규명했으며, 실험 쥐를 대상으로 한 연구에서 이 변이를 가진 쥐들은 상황이 바뀌어도 이전의 선택에 계속 집착하는 행동을 보였다. 연구진은 문제의 원인을 시상(thalamus)과 전전두피질(prefrontal cortex)을 연결하는 핵심 신경회로에 위치시켰다. 흥미롭게도 이 회로를 다시 활성화시키자 쥐들의 정상적인 행동이 회복되었다. 이 발견은 정신분열증 환자들의 인지적 경직성을 치료할 수 있는 새로운 치료법 개발에 희망을 제시하지만, 동물 실험 결과가 인간에게 동일하게 적용될지에 대한 추가 연구가 필요하다.

These findings offer hope for developing new therapeutic approaches to treat cognitive inflexibility in schizophrenia patients. However, further research is needed to determine whether these findings from animal models will translate to similar effects in humans, and the long-term efficacy of such interventions requires additional investigation.

articles.title: Eating more meat may lower Alzheimer’s risk for some people
articles.titleKo: 육류를 더 많이 섭취하면 일부 사람들의 알츠하이머병 위험이 낮아질 수 있다
articles.summary: A surprising new study suggests that genetics may change how diet affects brain health—especially when it comes to Alzheimer’s risk. Researchers found that older adults carrying high-risk APOE gene variants didn’t show the expected cognitive decline if they ate relatively high amounts of meat. In fact, those with these genes who consumed the most meat had slower cognitive decline and lower dementia risk, challenging conventional dietary advice.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigated how genetics may modify the relationship between diet and brain health, particularly regarding Alzheimer's risk. Researchers examined older adults carrying high-risk APOE gene variants and analyzed the relationship between meat consumption and cognitive function. Surprisingly, among those with high-risk genetic variants, individuals who consumed the most meat showed slower cognitive decline and lower dementia risk. This finding contradicts conventional dietary advice and suggests that the same food may have different effects on brain health depending on an individual's genetic profile. However, as an observational study, this research cannot definitively establish causation and requires further longitudinal investigation. These results emphasize the importance of personalized nutrition management and may contribute to the development of genetically-informed dietary interventions in the future.
